# services.tayga

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.tayga`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.tayga.dataDir` | `absolute path` | Directory for persistent data. |
| `services.tayga.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Tayga. |
| `services.tayga.ipv4` | `submodule` | IPv4-specific configuration. |
| `services.tayga.ipv4.address` | `null or string` | The source IPv4 address of the TAYGA server. |
| `services.tayga.ipv4.pool` | `null or (submodule)` | The pool of IPv4 addresses which are used for translation. |
| `services.tayga.ipv4.pool.address` | `string` | IPv4 address. |
| `services.tayga.ipv4.pool.prefixLength` | `integer between 0 and 32 (both inclusive)` | Subnet mask of the interface, specified as the number of bits in the prefix ("24"). |
| `services.tayga.ipv4.router.address` | `string` | The IPv4 address of the router. |
| `services.tayga.ipv6` | `submodule` | IPv6-specific configuration. |
| `services.tayga.ipv6.address` | `null or string` | The source IPv6 address of the TAYGA server. |
| `services.tayga.ipv6.pool` | `null or (submodule)` | The pool of IPv6 addresses which are used for translation. |
| `services.tayga.ipv6.pool.address` | `string` | IPv6 address. |
| `services.tayga.ipv6.pool.prefixLength` | `integer between 0 and 128 (both inclusive)` | Subnet mask of the interface, specified as the number of bits in the prefix ("64"). |
| `services.tayga.ipv6.router.address` | `string` | The IPv6 address of the router. |
| `services.tayga.log` | `list of string` | Packet errors to log (drop, reject, icmp, self) |
| `services.tayga.mappings` | `attribute set of string` | Static IPv4 -> IPv6 host mappings. |
| `services.tayga.package` | `package` | The tayga package to use. |
| `services.tayga.tunDevice` | `string` | Name of the nat64 tun device. |
| `services.tayga.wkpfStrict` | `boolean` | Enable restrictions on the use of the well-known prefix (64:ff9b::/96) - prevents translation of non-global IPv4 ranges when using the well-known prefix. Must be enabled for RFC 6052 compatibility. |
